Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria, suggesting significant renal impairment with possible diabetic ketoacidosis. Elevated ALT indicates some hepatic involvement. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, acidic pH) further support renal dysfunction. Immediate clinical evaluation for acute or chronic kidney disease and metabolic derangements is recommended.
